title: Battle of Anx Minor

{{Quote|…Admiral Ackbar scored a last-minute victory by focusing a hail of concentrated fire on the engines of the experimental vessel ''EX-F'' (also known as ''Glory of Yevetha''). The ship exploded, igniting its volatile antimatter reservoir and annihilating six nearby Star Destroyers.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
The '''Battle of Anx Minor''' was one of the last major battles of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Imperial Remnant]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], occurring during [[Imperial Skirmishes|final campaign]] of the Remnant in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]]. After a series of engagements, an [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] of Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] under the command of Remnant [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] engaged the New Republic [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third]] and [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|Fifth Fleets]] above the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Anx Minor/Legends|Anx Minor]], located deep within [[Imperial Space|Imperial territory]]. Commanding from the New Republic [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Guardian (Executor-class)|''Guardian'']], the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] inflicted heavy damage upon Pellaeon's [[Starship/Legends|ships]], forcing the Imperials to retreat. The defeat prompted Pellaeon and the Remnant to sue for peace [[19 ABY/Legends|two years later]], an initiative that brought about an official [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|end]] to the war.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|The New Republic added the Super Star Destroyer ''Guardian'' ''[…]'' to the third fleet, then sent both the Third and Fifth fleets out to engage Pellaeon in several major brawls.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
[[File:Pellaeon_Empire_map.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The New Republic and Imperial Remnant clashed one last time.]]
In [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], during the final stages of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Imperial Remnant]] under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] launched a [[Imperial Skirmishes|series of offensives]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], fearing that the Republic was preparing to invade [[Imperial Space]]. Although initial skirmishes at [[Battle of Adumar (17 ABY)|Adumar]], [[First battle of Taris|Taris]], and [[Battle of Champala|Champala]] proved successful for the Remnant, the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] quickly responded to the threat that Pellaeon's forces posed. The [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], commanding from the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Guardian (Executor-class)|''Guardian'']], led the [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third]] and [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|Fifth Fleets]] of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Navy]] into battle against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces, routing them at [[Second Battle of Champala|Champala]], [[Battle of Ketaris (17 ABY)|Ketaris]], and [[Battle of Tangrene (17 ABY)|Tangrene]]. With Pellaeon pushed back into the heart of Imperial territory as a result of these defeats, the stage was set for a final encounter between the opposing [[Commander/Legends|commanders]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==The battle==
The [[Fleet/Legends|armadas]] faced off in battle above [[Anx Minor/Legends|Anx Minor]], an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located near the core of Imperial territory.<ref name="Atlas" /> Ackbar, commanding from the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''Guardian'', led the New Republic Third and Fifth Fleets<ref name="NEGTC" /> into combat against Pellaeon's force of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]],<ref name="NEC" /> which included two [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnoughts]]—the [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] and the ''[[Megador]]''—and the [[Sorannan-class Star Destroyer|''Sorannan''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''Rakehell'']],<ref name="EGW" /> a weapons testbed. The resulting engagement between the two fleets proved to be the decisive end of the Imperial Remnant campaign.<ref name="NEC" />

Near the conclusion of the battle,<ref name="NEC" /> Ackbar ordered his forces to concentrate their fire on the ''Rakehell''. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] had determined a way to take out the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]]—there was a weak spot near its [[antimatter]] reservoir. Ackbar ordered his forces to target the armor surrounding the reservoir;<ref name="EGW" /> this barrage, coupled with attacks on the ship's [[Engine/Legends|engines]], destroyed the weapons testbed and ignited its antimatter reservoir. The resulting explosion was powerful enough to obliterate six Star Destroyers in the vicinity<ref name="NEC" /> and damage the ''Megador''.<ref name="EGW" /> Defeated, Pellaeon and the remaining Imperial ships fled the battle,<ref name="Atlas" /> retreating to the planet [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]].<ref name="EGW" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|When the dust from the conflict finally settled, Pellaeon's Empire had been pushed back into a mere eight sectors of a strategically barren section in the Outer Rim.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
[[File:Gavrisompellaeon.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The defeat suffered at Anx Minor was one of the factors that prompted Pellaeon to sue for peace two years later.]]
In the end, Pellaeon's gamble on a final, successful campaign against the New Republic failed, the combined forces of the New Republic Third and Fifth Fleet having destroyed a significant portion of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. The Imperial Remnant territory was reduced to eight Outer Rim [[Sector/Legends|sectors]],<ref name="NEGTC" /> and the [[Raioballo sector/Legends|Raioballo sector]], where Anx Minor was located,<ref name="Online Companion">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> was not one of them. This defeat, coupled with the Republic's close watch over the Remnant in subsequent [[Standard year/Legends|years]],<ref name="Atlas" /> prompted Pellaeon to assume direct command of the Remnant.<ref name="EGW" /> In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], two years after the defeat at Anx Minor, Pellaeon and the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] sued for peace; the Galactic Civil War was formally ended that year with the signing of the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Anx Minor was first mentioned in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and published in [[2002]]. In the book, the battle is described as having occurred after the [[Corellian Crisis|Corellian Insurrection]],<ref name="NEGTC" /> while later sources place the battle as happening before.<ref name="Atlas" /><ref name="NEC" /> The Battle of Anx Minor was later mentioned in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', and ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', none of which expanded upon the details of the conflict.<ref name="Atlas" /><ref name="NEC" /><ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', released in [[2012]], provided some new details on the battle.<ref name="EGW" />

Some confusion regarding the dating of the battle has existed: While ''The New Essential Guide to Characters'' originally supplied a date of 17 ABY ("[t]hirteen years after [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Endor]]"),<ref name="NEGTC" /> ''The New Essential Chronology'' proposed a generalization of 17–[[18 ABY/Legends|18 ABY]] for the Empire's final campaign (of which the Battle of Anx Minor was the final engagement),<ref name="NEC" /> and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' simply used 18 ABY for the date of the battle.<ref name="CSWE" /> ''The Essential Atlas'' and ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'', the most recent sources to mention the engagement, both went with the original 17 ABY dating.<ref name="Atlas" /><ref name="EGW" />
